title: The Appeal
content:
It I have given you delight
By aught that I have done,
Let me lie quiet in that night 
Which shall be yours anon:

And for the little, little, span
The dead are born in mind,
Seek not to question other than
The books I leave behind.
meaning:
Rudyard Kipling's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Appeal"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
